verification_questions.question: Is fast-check listed in devDependencies at ^3.23.0 or newer compatible?
verification_questions.check: node -e "const p = require('./package.json'); const v = p.devDependencies['fast-check']; if (!v || !v.startsWith('^3.')) { process.exit(1); } console.log('fast-check devDependency:', v);"

verification_questions.question: Does package-lock.json reflect the fast-check install at a resolved version >= 3.23.0?
verification_questions.check: node -e "const lock = require('./package-lock.json'); const entry = lock.packages['node_modules/fast-check']; if (!entry) { process.exit(1); } const [maj, min] = entry.version.split('.').map(Number); if (maj < 3 || (maj === 3 && min < 23)) { process.exit(1); } console.log('resolved fast-check version:', entry.version);"

verification_questions.question: Is the test-property target wired correctly in the Makefile (depends on node_modules, sets FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000, targets both property test files, uses --passWithNoTests, is .PHONY, appears between test and dev, has a ## doc comment)?
Does make test still pass with 9 files / 48 tests (same as baseline)?
Does make test-property exit 0 cleanly even though the property test files do not yet exist?
Does a deliberately failing property test (e.g. expect(1).toBe(2) inside fc.property) cause make test-property to exit non-zero? (One-shot manual check; not committed.)
verification_questions.check: Manually add a stub test to src/store/task-reducer.property.test.ts with a failing assertion, run make test-property, observe non-zero exit, then delete the stub.

verification_questions.question: Are no production source files modified — only package.json, package-lock.json, and Makefile?
